Amir Lehbab
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2|  J: David Probert|  T: A M Balding
15/2
77

Foaled April 5. 200,000 gns No Nay Never brother to 2-y-o 6f-7f winner Never Fear and useful 2-y-o 6f winner Rudi's Apple. Dam 7.5f/1m winner. Seemingly deserted by Oisin Murphy but still rates an interesting runner.

Foaled February 9. 150,000 gns Blue Point half-brother to 1m/9f winner I Am Me. The betting should help guide to expectations.

Cavalier
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2|  J: S M Levey|  T: R Hannon
10/1
75

Foaled March , €95,000 Minzaal colt. Half-brother to 7f winner They All Know Me. Dam, 6f winner (including at 2 yrs), half-sister to useful winner up to 6f Dave Dexter. One of 3 newcomers here for the Richard Hannon yard and he's the mount of Sean Levey.

Cloud Summit
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2|  J: Billy Loughnane|  T: G Boughey
6/1
73

Foaled February 21. 100,000 gns No Nay Never colt. Dam, runner-up at 1m in US from 2 starts, half-sister to 1000 Guineas/Poule d'Essai des Pouliches winner Special Duty. Leading yard won a division of this race last year.

Herbert Hall
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2|  J: Oisin Murphy|  T: B J Meehan

76

Foaled April 27. £36,000 Starman colt. Dam, maiden (stayed 7.5f), sister to useful 7f-2m winner Agitare. The booking of Oisin Murphy catches the eye.

Jimtrott

Foaled April 3. 110,000 gns No Nay Never colt. Dam French 9.4f winner. The stable has very promising dual winner Night In Vegas to compare this one to so a market move would be interesting.

Keep Grating

75

Foaled March 6. €35,000 Space Traveller colt. Half-brother to several winners, including winner up to 6f Dazzling Des and 6f/7f winner Merry Secret. The yard may have stronger contenders elsewhere based on jockey arrangements.

Night Star
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2|  J: P J Dobbs|  T: R Hannon

75

Foaled April 3. £60,000 Space Traveller half-brother to 6f winner Smoke Them Out. Dam 6f winner. Another of the Hannon newcomers. The market should provide more clues.

Top Rated in Race
Top Rated in Race
62p

100/30, badly in need of experience when fifth of 6 in novice at Doncaster (6f, good to firm, 10/3) on debut 40 days ago. Should have more to offer for top yard.

Percy Shaw
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2|  J: Jason Watson|  T: C Hills
11/1
81

Foaled February 14. 40,000 gns Bated Breath colt. Brother to useful 5f winner Lady Tilbury and half-brother to 6f winner Rinky Tinky Tinky. Dam, 5f winner, half-sister to smart winner up to 7f Nufoos. The stable won this race with a newcomer in 2023.

9/2, remote fourth of 6 in maiden at Lingfield (7f, good to firm, 9/2) on debut 24 days ago. Can only watch after that.

Foaled April 27. €200,000 Gleneagles colt. Dam, French 10.5f winner, half-sister to very smart winner up to 1m Aclaim. Another for whom the betting should help guide to expectations.

Sole Ambition
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2|  J: K Shoemark|  T: E Walker
9/2
79

Foaled February 4. 210,000 gns No Nay Never colt. Dam 6f winner, granddaughter of Nell Gwyn/Princess Margaret Stakes winner Scarlet Runner. The yard has had 2-y-o winners in recent days. Would firmly enter calculations if the betting suggests he's fancied.

Non-Runners

1
(2)
Al Qayed
Weight: 9-7|  Age: 2
T: W J Haggas  J: Non Runner
NR

Forecasts

Sole Ambition (7/2), Al Qayed (5/1), Amir Lehbab (11/2), Cloud Summit (6/1), Silver Dominion (7/1), Cavalier (10/1), Arabian Blue (12/1), Jimtrott (12/1), Night Star (12/1), Keep Grating (14/1), Note To Self (14/1), Herbert Hall (16/1), Percy Shaw (16/1), Rafe's da Man (20/1)

A race where the betting will be informative. Before any such clues are known the suggestion is SOLE AMBITION. Amir Lehbab, Herbert Hall, for whom Oisin Murphy is an interesting booking, and Cavalier, the mount of Sean Levey from the Richard Hannon team, are other newcomers who make paper appeal. The Gosden stable's Note To Self could also prove a different proposition with a run behind him.

  1. Sole Ambition
    9/2
  2. Amir Lehbab
    15/2
  3. Herbert Hall
    7/1

Terms and ConditionsPrivacy PolicyPrivacy StatementPrivacy Preference CentreCookie Policy
© 2026 Hestview Ltd. All Rights Reserved.
We are committed to Safer Gambling and have a number of self-help tools to help you manage your gambling. We also work with a number of independent charitable organisations who can offer help and answers any questions you may have.

Sporting Life+ racecards powered by Timeform

By clicking “Allow All Cookies”, you agree to the storing of cookies on your device to enhance site navigation, analyze site usage, and assist in our marketing efforts. Sporting Life Cookie Policy

Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
More information

Manage Consent Preferences

Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are strictly necessary to enable you to navigate our websites and to use the features you have requested during the course of your gaming experience. They are used to provide content to you, to provide products and services that you have requested and to provide a secure experience. You can set your browser or device platform to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the product will not then work.

Performance Cookies

These cookies are used to collect information on website usage. Such cookies are used to provide statistics on visitors to our websites. These statistics are used for internal purposes to help us to test and improve the performance of our websites, providing a better user experience.

Third Party Marketing / Targeting Cookies

These cookies may be set through our website by us or by our third party partners. They may be used by us or by our third party partners to store information about your preferences and to show you relevant adverts on our website or our third party partner’s websites, to personalise our website, to tailor our marketing communications to you and to measure and improve our performance. These cookies may not contain any information that personally identifies you, but other personal data that we store about you may be linked to the information stored in and obtained from the cookies. You can choose to disable these cookies, but you will experience content and adverts that are less relevant to you.
